codeReview.summary: The original blocking gap from commit 8515a02 is now resolved by the fix chain 8acf264 -> 3afe3b6 -> 1b82807. The checker no longer relies solely on arm-qualified patterns, now propagates authoritative enum type information through identifiers, annotations, function returns, and block final expressions, and the latest fix removes unsound if/match branch sampling so whole-expression enum inference only uses authoritative sources. Current code in lib/exhaust.ml still falls back to arm-pattern enum extraction for direct matches, which preserves expected rejection of non-exhaustive qualified matches without reintroducing bogus cached types. I did not find a remaining correctness issue within the reviewed failure scope.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The mission and compiler-worker skill instruct workers to read `.factory/services.yaml`, but this repo does not contain that file, so workers repeatedly fall back to mission AGENTS.md or ad-hoc command discovery.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: The phase-6 whole-expression fix transcript explicitly attempted to read `/home/lenstr/workspace/personal/hack/rgo-lang/.factory/services.yaml`, while this review found the repo path absent and only the mission AGENTS.md listed the validator commands.

summary: Reviewed the original failed feature (8515a02), the fix chain commits 8acf264, 3afe3b6, and 1b82807, the fix handoff/transcript for worker session 4a9eff5c-fcb1-43bd-8ffb-aa7417793b91, and the current `lib/exhaust.ml`/`test/test_exhaust.ml`. The original failure is adequately resolved: authoritative scrutinee typing now covers the earlier skipped enum matches, block-produced enum values retain their type information, and whole-expression enum inference no longer samples a single if-branch or match arm. The latest regression coverage specifically exercises those cases, so this re-run passes.
